title: lyonia marginata var. rubra
intro: Lyonia marginata var. rubra, also known as Red Fringed Lyonia, is a shrub native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its red-fringed leaves and white flowers. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and bark have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, including astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Red Fringed Lyonia include treating skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as reducing fever and relieving pain. Red Fringed Lyonia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal properties, although caution is advised due to potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
